channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Driver in crash that killed 4 kids was allegedly photographing ‘Welcome to Florida’ sign
item.description: A New York man was extradited to Florida this month after he was accused of causing a deadly crash when he came to a near-stop on Interstate 95 to take photo of the "Welcome to Florida" sign.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners who let nearly 190 bodies decay plead guilty to corpse abuse
item.description: COLORADO SPRINGS, Colo. (AP) — The owners of a Colorado funeral home who let nearly 190 bodies decay in a room-temperature building and gave grieving families fake ashes pleaded guilty on Friday to corpse abuse.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas education board approves optional Bible-infused curriculum for elementary schools
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas’ education board voted Friday to allow Bible-infused teachings in elementary schools under optional new curriculum that could test boundaries between religion and public classrooms in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NATO and Ukraine to hold emergency talks after Russia's attack with new hypersonic missile
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — NATO and Ukraine will hold emergency talks Tuesday after Russia attacked a central city with an experimental, hypersonic ballistic missile that escalated the nearly 33-month-old war.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and VOLODYMYR YURCHUK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump may move Space Command from Colorado to Alabama
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to move U.S. Space Command from Colorado Springs, Colorado to Huntsville, Alabama.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, ABC 4

item.title: Las Vegas locals will not be shocked to learn which city ranked 'Most Sinful' in 2024
item.description: Denver was ranked as the No. 6 most "sinful" city in the U.S., ahead of cities like New York City and Miami in a new ranking released by WalletHub.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Matt Gaetz says he won't return to Congress next year after withdrawing name for attorney general
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former Rep. Matt Gaetz said Friday that he will not be returning to Congress after withdrawing his name from consideration to be attorney general under President-elect Donald Trump amid growing allegations of sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Removal of Pride tree inside library draws controversy from residents
item.description: A Christmas tree that displayed support for the LGBTQ+ community was recently taken down at a public library.
item.dc:creator: Sam Chimenti, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz says he will not return to Congress
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) said Friday he will not return to Congress after resigning his House seat last week, despite having withdrawn from consideration to be President-elect Trump’s attorney general — but added he is “not going anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuelan migrant arrested in Colorado for allegedly sexually assaulting boss' 14-year-old daughter: report
item.description: A Venezuelan migrant is accused of sexually assaulting the 14-year-old daughter of his employer while he lived in the individual's house in Colorado, police and reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump immigration crackdown: Denaturalization just a drop in the bucket
item.description: President-elect Trump’s incoming administration is expected to take aim at legal immigration, in addition to cracking down on the illegal variety, slowing the pace of application approvals and redirecting resources to look for fraud in old applications, including naturalizations.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KRON4

item.title: The 61st anniversary of JFK's assassination
item.description: More than six decades since the assassination of President John F. Kennedy, questions remain as to what truly occurred.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Clonazepam tablets recalled over 'life-threatening' label mistake
item.description: The company that produces the tablets has said the medication is mislabeled with the incorrect strength on the carton.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KRON4

item.title: Cases of 'walking pneumonia' are rising across the US: Here's where it's worst
item.description: In October, the CDC wrote that since late spring, the number of infections caused by walking pneumonia has been increasing, especially among young children.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Rousseau, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Storm inundates Northern California with rain, heavy snow. Thousands remain in the dark in Seattle
item.description: Heavy rain from a major storm prompted evacuation warnings for communities near a Northern California river that forecasters say could break its banks Friday, as the system continued to dump heavy snow in mountainous areas where some ski resorts opened for the season.
item.dc:creator: Godofredo A. Vásquez, Janie Har And Christopher Weber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What's next for Matt Gaetz: 5 possibilities
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz’s (R-Fla.) political future is up in the air following his announcement that he is taking his name out of the running to be attorney general in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Historically bad year for dolphin strandings on Cape Cod has scientists looking for answers
item.description: An unprecedentedly bad year for beached dolphins on Cape Cod might have to do with warming waters changing the availability of the animals' food, said scientists hoping to curb the strandings.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 73% worry Social Security won't pay promised benefits: Survey
item.description: Most nonretired U.S. adults, 73%, are worried they won't get their promised Social Security benefits when they retire.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Number of women who are state lawmakers inches up to a record high
item.description: Women will for the first time make up a majority of state legislators in Colorado and New Mexico next year, but at least 13 states saw losses in female representation after the November election, according to a count released Thursday by the Rutgers Center for American Women and Politics.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missing father of 3 did 'research' to stage his own death and flee the country: sheriff
item.description: Wisconsin officials have successfully contacted Ryan Borgwardt, a missing father of three who "staged his death" in August and apparently fled the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Disneyland Paris turns into real-life winter wonderland after season's 1st snowfall
item.description: The season’s first snowfall has turned Disneyland Paris into a real-life winter wonderland. Disney Parks shared a video on Thursday showing snow covering Main Street, U.S.A, Sleeping Beauty Castle, and all the festive holiday décor. It’s currently pouring snow at Disneyland Paris. pic.twitter.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Kit Kats to come in new shape for the first time ever in America
item.description: The Hershey Company has debuted Kit Kats in the shape of something other than a traditional Kit Kat "finger.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, KRON4

item.title: The FBI arrested a man who's been charged with planning an attack on the New York Stock Exchange
item.description: A Florida man was arrested Wednesday and charged with a plot to “reboot” the U.S. government by planting a bomb at the New York Stock Exchange this week and detonating it with a remote-controlled device, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Attack of the trees: Aging giants wreak havoc in Missouri neighborhood
item.description: "We’re pretty concerned. We’re thinking, ‘Should we move before the trees destroy our home?’” said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KRON4

item.title: Not allowed: AMC issues strict warning to 'Wicked' fans ahead of film's premiere
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: Nvidia beats earnings expectations as investors eye demand for Blackwell AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in third-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized computer chips that power artificial intelligence systems remains robust. For the three months that ended Oct.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Court ruling stops Louisiana from requiring Ten Commandments in classrooms for now
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Louisiana's plan to make all of the state's public school classrooms post the Ten Commandments next year remains on hold under an order Wednesday by a federal appeals court in New Orleans. The 5th U.S.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Survivors of LGBTQ+ club shooting say the deaths and trauma could've been avoided in lawsuits
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Adriana Vance collected herself in front of the cameras, two years to the day after her son was killed in the mass shooting at an LGBTQ+ nightclub in Colorado Springs, a murder that lawsuits allege could have been prevented by law enforcement and the club's owners.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California governor will not make clemency decision for Menendez brothers until new DA reviews case
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom said he won't make a clemency decision on the murder convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez until newly elected Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman reviews the nearly 35-year-old case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump chooses TV doctor Mehmet Oz to lead Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump says he is nominating Dr. Mehmet Oz, who hosted a long-running television talk show, to lead the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 hurt when plane crashes near Southern California dragstrip hosting racing event
item.description: At least two parked vehicles on the ground were struck when the small plane crashed around near the dragstrip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
